items.evidence: Root cause in controller/pdf_controller.py:2316-2329 `_invalidate_thumbnails`: the count-CHANGED branch (delete/insert) calls `self.view.set_thumbnail_placeholders(n)` which (view/pdf_view.py:2309-2317) does `thumbnail_list.clear()` and re-adds LABEL-ONLY items (`頁{i+1}`, no icon) for ALL rows, but then schedules re-render only from `start = max(0, min(affected)-2)` (line 2328) to end. Rows [0, start) therefore keep the blank label placeholder forever. Delete a page anywhere past the top and every thumbnail above min(deleted)-2 vanishes. Secondary: for dirty sessions `_resolve_thumbnail_file_source` returns None (controller:2362) so it falls to the one-page-per-event-loop live path (thumbnail_coordinator.py:201-217); a session switch/next op bumps `_next_thumb_gen` and cancels mid-run, leaving trailing rows blank. PITFALLS_INDEX.md:123 documents this exact count-changed/unchanged contract.
items.approach: In the count-changed else-branch of `_invalidate_thumbnails`, render from 0 (set `start=0`) since `set_thumbnail_placeholders` just blanked every icon — this matches the initial-open path (controller:926). Better: preserve unaffected icons instead of full `clear()` (only remove/insert the affected rows in `update_thumbnail_batch`/a new view method) so pages before the edit never blank. Keep the existing generation-token cancellation (`_schedule_thumbnail_batch`, `_thumbnail_identity_matches`).
items.risk: Rendering all rows after every delete is slightly heavier for huge docs, but equals current open-path cost and is async/cancellable. If instead preserving icons, must correctly shift icons for rows after the deletion — mirror `block_manager.shift_after_delete` semantics.
items.reuse: `_schedule_thumbnail_batch`, `_next_thumb_gen`, generation tokens, `update_thumbnail_batch` (view:2319), open-path precedent at controller:926.

items.evidence: view/pdf_view.py:335 `self.setMinimumSize(1280, 800)` hard-floors the window well above 720x520. Sidebars sit in a QSplitter (`main_splitter`, line 380) but `left_sidebar.setMinimumWidth(200)` (387) and `right_sidebar.setMinimumWidth(240)` (441) prevent them collapsing; no `setCollapsible`/`setChildrenCollapsible` calls exist (grep empty). No `resizeEvent` override on PDFView (grep empty), so nothing auto-collapses sidebars when the window narrows.
items.approach: Lower `setMinimumSize` to (720,520). Drop sidebar `setMinimumWidth` floors (or set to 0) and rely on `main_splitter` collapsibility; add a `resizeEvent` (or reuse `_apply_sidebar_sizes`) that auto-hides both sidebars via existing `toggle_left_sidebar`/`toggle_right_sidebar` when width < a threshold, restoring `_left_sidebar_last_width`/`_right_sidebar_last_width` when widened. Diagonal corner resize is native to QMainWindow once the min-size floor is removed.
items.risk: Ribbon toolbar `_recompute_ribbon_text_min_width` (called in apply_theme:1004) and the status-bar theme switcher may impose an implicit min width; verify the shell actually reaches 720. Auto-collapse thresholds can fight manual splitter drags.
items.reuse: `_apply_sidebar_sizes` (1435), `toggle_left_sidebar`/`toggle_right_sidebar` (1474/1489), `_resolve_sidebar_width`, `_left_sidebar_last_width` (472).

items.evidence: `_update_thumbnail_layout_metrics` (view/pdf_view.py:2234-2273) already centers the column via `setViewportMargins(horizontal_margin,…)` (2262) and scales icon/grid to viewport width (`item_w=min(available_w,280)`, `icon_w=max(96,item_w-18)`, 2251-2265); driven on sidebar resize by the eventFilter viewport-Resize hook (2229-2231). Git `a243e8c` 'center thumbnail column in wide sidebars' and `1404f7e` 'one thumbnail per row' already landed; PITFALLS_INDEX.md:31 records the 'wide sidebars should center not stretch' rule; tests assert it in test_scripts/test_multi_tab_plan.py:465-522.
items.approach: Requirement appears already satisfied (doc may be stale). If residual whitespace remains, tune the `max_item_w = 280` cap (line 2249) and the `icon_w = item_w - 18` / `item_h = icon_h + 28` padding (2253-2259) so the icon fills more of each cell.
items.risk: Very low; only cosmetic constant tuning. Changing caps affects test_multi_tab_plan assertions (grid width <=280, icon width >=0.75*viewport).
items.reuse: `_update_thumbnail_layout_metrics`, `_thumbnail_icon_aspect_ratio` (2275).

items.evidence: Continuous-mode pages are laid out left-aligned: `initialize_continuous_placeholders` sets `item.setPos(0, y)` (view/pdf_view.py:2595) and `scene.setSceneRect(0,0,max_w,y)` (2602), so a page narrower than the widest page hugs x=0. No explicit `graphics_view.setAlignment(Qt.AlignCenter)` (grep empty) — QGraphicsView's default centers the whole scene only when it is smaller than the viewport, not individual narrow pages within the column. Single-page path centers via `centerOn` (2644/2668) and `scroll_to_page` centers vertically (2537).
items.approach: Center each page horizontally by positioning items at `x=(max_w - width_scene)/2` in `initialize_continuous_placeholders` and `update_page_in_scene`, and/or set `graphics_view.setAlignment(Qt.AlignHCenter|Qt.AlignVCenter)`. Re-center on window resize (add resizeEvent) and on sidebar toggle.
items.risk: Hit-testing/coordinate transforms assume page x0=0 (e.g. `page_x0 = 0.0` at 3875, click-to-page math, `_scene_y_to_page_index`); shifting item x requires updating all scene<->page x conversions or the wrong page/rect gets picked. This is the main regression surface.
items.reuse: `page_y_positions`/`page_heights` layout loop, `update_page_in_scene`, `centerOn` usage.

items.evidence: `document_tab_bar.setTabsClosable(True)` (view/pdf_view.py:885) with `setElideMode(Qt.ElideMiddle)` (887). QSS in view/theme.py:480-485 only sets `border-radius:4px` and a hover background on `QTabBar#documentTabBar::close-button` — it does NOT set an image or size, so the button uses the Qt style's default close icon, which under many styles is faint/hover-dependent and smaller than 20x20.
items.approach: In build_qss extend `QTabBar#documentTabBar::close-button` with an explicit themed X `image: url(...)`, `width:20px; height:20px; subcontrol-position: right; margin-left:6px;` and ensure a `:!selected` state keeps it painted (add `::close-button:!selected { image: … }`). Provide the X asset via view/icons.py. Elide already keeps the title clear of the close button since it sits at the tab's right subcontrol.
items.risk: Need a crisp X glyph asset that works in all three themes (light/dark); pure-QSS default icon can't be forced always-on in every style without an explicit image. Widening the close button eats into min tab width (120px, theme.py:460).
items.reuse: view/icons.py icon infrastructure, theme tokens (`t["pressed"]`, etc.), existing close-button QSS block.

items.evidence: Both dialogs iterate `_PAGE_ROTATION_SCOPE_LABELS` = (ALL, CURRENT, ODD, EVEN) (view/pdf_view.py:256-261): delete `_prompt_delete_page_scope` (4480-4483) and rotate `_prompt_page_rotation_options` (4561-4563). Neither offers CUSTOM. But `_pages_for_scope` (4500-4522) ALREADY implements `_PAGE_SCOPE_CUSTOM` via `QInputDialog.getText` + `parse_pages` (4513-4521) and `_PAGE_SCOPE_CURRENT` (4505). `parse_pages` (utils/helpers.py:6-22) parses/clamps '1,3-5' (1-based) and raises on non-numeric. `_PAGE_SCOPE_LABELS` (249-255) already lists CUSTOM but is unused.
items.approach: Delete dialog: feed a new tuple (ALL, ODD, EVEN, CUSTOM) — removes 目前頁 and adds 自訂範圍. Rotate dialog: add `_PAGE_SCOPE_CUSTOM` to its combo. No new parsing needed; `_pages_for_scope` already validates before mutation and `_delete_pages_for_scope`/`_rotate_pages_for_scope` only emit when pages is non-empty (4526-4527/4583-4584).
items.risk: Low. parse_pages silently clamps out-of-range to empty rather than erroring — acceptable ('validate before mutation' honored since empty→no emit). Watch tests that assume the current label set / monkeypatch parse_pages (test_thumbnail_context_menu.py:153).
items.reuse: `_pages_for_scope`, `_PAGE_SCOPE_CUSTOM`/`_PAGE_SCOPE_LABELS`, `parse_pages`, existing sig_delete_pages/sig_rotate_pages.

items.evidence: `search_input.returnPressed` connects to `_trigger_search` (view/pdf_view.py:1547, 4786-4790) which unconditionally re-emits `sig_search` (a fresh full search) on every Enter. Next/prev navigation already exists with wrap-around: `_navigate_search_next` (2728-2732) uses modulo and `_navigate_search_previous` (2722-2726); prev/next buttons wired (1553-1555); `_jump_to_search_index` (2734-2739).
items.approach: Track the last-searched query; in `_trigger_search`, if the query is unchanged and `self.current_search_results` is populated, call `self._navigate_search_next()` (which already wraps) instead of re-emitting `sig_search`. First Enter with a new/changed query still searches. Wrap is already implemented per requirement.
items.risk: Async search may still be streaming results (search_coordinator appends incrementally); guard on non-empty `current_search_results` and reset the 'last query' when the query text changes so a re-typed query re-searches.
items.reuse: `_navigate_search_next`, `current_search_index`, `_jump_to_search_index`, existing next/prev buttons.

items.evidence: No page move/reorder API in the model (grep found none; `model/merge_session.py` reorder is for merge inputs only). `thumbnail_list` disables reordering: `setMovement(QListView.Static)` (view/pdf_view.py:1533), no drag flags. Undo uses SnapshotCommand (model/edit_commands.py:318; delete/rotate build it at controller:1484-1493/1508-1516). Stale-index infra exists for structural ops: `block_manager.shift_after_insert`/`shift_after_delete` (model/text_block.py:70,89) + `list_stale_pages` (66) + `_schedule_stale_index_drain` (used by delete, controller:1496). Merge dialog is the reorder prior art: `file_list.setDragDropMode(QAbstractItemView.InternalMove)` (view/dialogs/merge.py:43).
items.approach: (1) Model: add `move_page(from_idx,to_idx)` using `doc.move_page` + mark both affected indices/range stale (new `shift_after_move` mirroring shift_after_delete). (2) Controller: `reorder_page(from,to)` capturing `_capture_doc_snapshot` before/after, record `SnapshotCommand(command_type="move_page")`, then `_invalidate_thumbnails`, `_rebuild_continuous_scene`, `_schedule_stale_index_drain` (same sequence as delete_pages). Saved output follows model doc order automatically. (3) View: enable `InternalMove` (or a custom drop) on `thumbnail_list` and emit a new `sig_reorder_page(from,to)` on drop — View must NOT mutate the model (signals only).
items.risk: Index/stale correctness across the moved range; undo snapshot memory (SnapshotCommand byte budget); must coordinate with the FIX-thumbnails-vanish rebuild so reordered thumbnails repaint. `setMovement(Static)`→drag may conflict with the existing thumbnail context menu / click-to-page.
items.reuse: SnapshotCommand + delete_pages sequence (controller:1475-1497), `shift_after_delete` pattern, merge InternalMove prior art, `_schedule_stale_index_drain`.

items.evidence: `document_tab_bar` (view/pdf_view.py:880-890) sets NO `setContextMenuPolicy`/`customContextMenuRequested` — only `thumbnail_list` (1535-1538) and `graphics_view` (561) have context menus. Tab→session mapping exists: `setTabData(idx, meta["id"])` (946) and `setTabToolTip(idx, meta["path"] or title)` (947). Session file path resolvable via `model.get_session_meta` (`saved_path`/`path`, used at controller:2364-2365). No existing Explorer/`/select` code anywhere (grep empty).
items.approach: Set `document_tab_bar.setContextMenuPolicy(Qt.CustomContextMenu)` and connect `customContextMenuRequested` to a handler that maps `tabAt(pos)`→session id (tabData) and builds a QMenu with exactly one item '開啟檔案所在位置'. Resolve the path through a controller query facade (respect layering), then run `subprocess.run(['explorer', '/select,', path])` on Windows. Disable the item when the session has no saved path.
items.risk: Unsaved/new tabs have no path (disable/greyed). Cross-platform: `/select,` is Windows-only (macOS `open -R`, Linux none). Launching Explorer from the View is an OS action, not a model mutation, so layering is respected as long as the path comes via the controller query facade.
items.reuse: tab tooltip/tabData, `model.get_session_meta` / controller read-only query facade, `_show_thumbnail_context_menu` as a QMenu template.

items.evidence: Ctrl+W exists ONLY on macOS: the 'Close Tab' QAction with `QKeySequence.StandardKey.Close` (view/pdf_view.py:1317-1319) is created inside `_build_macos_menu_bar`, which returns early when `sys.platform != 'darwin'` (1360-1363). `_install_document_tab_shortcuts` (892-920) has no Ctrl+W. The full close pipeline already exists cross-platform: `_close_current_document_tab` (978-985) → `sig_tab_close_requested` → `on_tab_close_requested` (controller:1395-1430) with unsaved prompt `_confirm_close_session` (1371-1393). Closing the last tab already lands on `_reset_empty_ui` (894-900), and Ctrl+O (`_action_open`) is registered as WidgetWithChildrenShortcut + `addAction` (1278-1290) so it still fires on the empty shell.
items.approach: Add `QShortcut(QKeySequence('Ctrl+W'), self)` with `WidgetWithChildrenShortcut` context in `_install_document_tab_shortcuts`, connected to `_close_current_document_tab`. Empty-window-after-last-tab and Ctrl+O-still-works already hold via `_reset_empty_ui` + the always-added open action.
items.risk: Avoid double-binding on macOS where StandardKey.Close is already Cmd+W (guard or accept duplicate — Qt tolerates). Ensure the shortcut is inactive/no-op when no tabs are open.
items.reuse: `_close_current_document_tab`, `on_tab_close_requested`, `_confirm_close_session`, `_reset_empty_ui`, `_install_document_tab_shortcuts`.

items.evidence: `PDFView.keyPressEvent` (view/pdf_view.py:2176-2197) only handles Ctrl+Tab direction, Esc, Ctrl+C, Delete/Backspace, Ctrl+F — no PageUp/PageDown/Home/End (grep for Key_PageUp/Key_Home/Key_End is empty). Navigation API is available: `scroll_to_page(page_idx)` (called at 2608), `current_page`, `total_pages`, continuous `page_y_positions`, and `page_number_input.returnPressed`→jump (1231).
items.approach: Extend `keyPressEvent` (or add QShortcuts) so PageDown→`scroll_to_page(current_page+1)`, PageUp→`current_page-1`, Home→0, End→`total_pages-1`, then `event.accept()`. Guard: skip when `self.text_editor` is active or when `search_input`/`page_number_input` (or any QLineEdit) has focus, and restrict to browse-ish modes.
items.risk: QGraphicsView/its scrollbar already consumes PageUp/PageDown for pixel scrolling — must intercept before super() and `accept()` to avoid double movement; must not steal keys from focused text inputs / inline editor.
items.reuse: `scroll_to_page`, `current_page`/`total_pages`, existing keyPressEvent dispatch and mode/focus guards (`_finalize_text_edit`, `current_mode`).

items.evidence: Architecture is single-process/one-window: main.py builds exactly one `QApplication`, one `PDFView` (76), one `PDFModel`+`PDFController` (96-98); the single-instance server (main.py:120-127, utils/single_instance) makes a 2nd process forward its paths and exit rather than open a window. Tabs are session switches inside ONE model — `document_tab_bar.setMovable(False)` (view/pdf_view.py:884); the controller assumes a single active/rendered session throughout (`get_active_session_id`, per-session UI state dicts, worker-snapshot cache keyed by one sid at controller:1414). No detach/drag-out code exists (git grep empty).
items.approach: True drag-off-the-tabbar detach is not supported by today's model (one active session, non-serializable `CommandManager` undo stack, single-active-session assumptions). Minimal feasible MVP: a right-click/menu 'Open in new window' that (a) spins up a SECOND in-process PDFView+PDFController+PDFModel triple (single-instance is per-process, so multiple windows are legal), (b) transfers the dragged session's bytes via `capture_worker_snapshot_bytes`/save into the new model + reopens, carrying page/zoom/dirty from its `_session_ui_state`, then (c) `close_session` on the origin. Set `document_tab_bar.setMovable(True)` and add a drag-leave detector only if the drag-out gesture is required.
items.risk: Undo/redo stack (edit_commands.py:413 CommandManager) is not transferable across models, so detached window would lose undo history unless snapshot-serialized; worker-snapshot cache, thumbnail generation maps, and single-active-session assumptions in the controller all need per-window isolation. Honest recommendation: DESCOPE the live tab-drag; ship 'Open in new window' transferring one session (dirty/page/zoom) as the pragmatic subset, or defer entirely.
items.reuse: `capture_worker_snapshot_bytes`, `model.save_as`/`open_pdf`, session APIs (`get_session_meta`, `close_session`), `_session_ui_state`; PDFView already constructs cleanly with `defer_heavy_panels`.

view-shell.notes: Git history shows several thumbnail items already landed and may make the requirements doc stale: `a243e8c` (center thumbnail column in wide sidebars) and `1404f7e` (one thumbnail per row) => AC-MOD-02 is effectively DONE; `9e4b4de` added the thumbnail page-op context menu. No prior work exists for page reorder (only merge-input reorder, `bd92ece`) or tab detach (git grep empty), confirming AC-NEW-06 and AC-NEW-03 are greenfield. PITFALLS_INDEX.md:123 ('Thumbnail invalidation must distinguish count-changed from count-unchanged', pointing at `_invalidate_thumbnails`/`_schedule_thumbnail_batch`/`update_thumbnail_batch`) is the exact contract broken by FIX-thumbnails-vanish — the count-changed branch full-clears icons then re-renders only from min(affected)-2, so pages above that index stay label-only. Layering to preserve in all fixes: View emits signals only (sig_reorder_page, sig_tab_close_requested, tab-context signal), the Controller is the sole mutation coordinator (delete/rotate/move via model + SnapshotCommand), and all background rasterization stays QThread+Signals (thumbnail_coordinator / search_coordinator already follow this). Reusable range-parsing helpers already exist in two places: utils/helpers.py:parse_pages (1-based '1,3-5', used by delete/rotate/export/watermark) and utils/ocr_types.py:parse_page_range (0-based, supports 'all'/default-current, well-tested in test_scripts/test_ocr_types.py) — prefer parse_pages for AC-NEW-01 to match the existing dialog path.

items.evidence: Startup is already heavily lazified: main.py:65-68 imports PySide6/view.icons/pdf_view inside run() (not module top); main.py:76 PDFView(defer_heavy_panels=not cli_args) builds an empty shell that defers heavy panels when no CLI file; main.py:89-104,118 build PDFModel+PDFController lazily on view.sig_backend_bootstrap_requested (deferred imports at 93-94); PDFController.__init__ (controller/pdf_controller.py:198-238) only allocates coordinators/dicts, and activate() (244-259) just connects bridges/signals behind an idempotent self._activated guard. File-open (open_pdf 955 -> model.pdf_model.open_pdf:681) is synchronous on the GUI thread and does a conditional XREF-repair round-trip (_repair_doc_xref_in_memory:644, doc.tobytes(garbage=1):666 - O(file size)); the full text index is NOT built on open (pdf_model.py:782-783 comment; lazy via ensure_page_index_built:792). First visible page is rendered immediately (pdf_controller.py:932 _schedule_visible_render immediate low-res then high); thumbnails+sidebar scans are deferred by OPEN_BACKGROUND_FALLBACK_MS=250 (69,933-936) or triggered after first high render (_maybe_start_background_loading_after_render:392-400). Prior perf commits: 5216ea5 (defer numpy/dialog imports), 6da81b8 (drop deflate from XREF repair ~9x), 60c36fc (async thumbnails).
items.approach: Three biggest remaining deferrals: (1) The primary page raster is still synchronous on the GUI thread - _render_page_into_scene (pdf_controller.py:717-746) calls model.get_page_pixmap (pdf_model.py:1522) inline; move it to a QThread worker mirroring controller/thumbnail_coordinator.py (_ThumbnailWorker moveToThread at 167-169) and reuse the existing _render_gen_by_session token + _render_cache for invalidation. (2) The XREF-repair tobytes round-trip (pdf_model.py:666) runs on the GUI thread for damaged files - gate it more tightly or offload. (3) Confirm the pdf_controller.py:37 module-level `from model.tools.ocr_tool import is_device_available` stays cheap (ocr_tool.py:36-50 defers torch/surya via importlib) - it does, so no change needed there. Launch-shell time is already near-optimal; the file-open first-paint is the real lever via (1).
items.risk: Threading the primary render risks races with the current synchronous scene updates; mitigated by reusing the existing per-session generation tokens (_next_render_gen) and cache keying (_render_cache_key) already built for this.
items.reuse: controller/thumbnail_coordinator.py QThread+Signal worker pattern; _render_gen_by_session/_render_cache; OPEN_BACKGROUND_FALLBACK_MS deferral idiom; git 5216ea5/6da81b8 perf approach.

items.evidence: Render pipeline: pdf_controller._render_page_into_scene:717-746 -> model.get_page_pixmap:1522 -> tools.render_page_pixmap -> fitz page.get_pixmap(matrix=Matrix(scale,scale)) - executed SYNCHRONOUSLY on the GUI thread for the visible/primary page. First page IS prioritized over thumbnails: _render_active_session:932 renders the initial page immediately (low quality via LOW_RES_RENDER_SCALE=0.5 at 68/711-715, then high), while thumbnails wait for the 250ms fallback or the first high-res render (933-936, 392-400). Thumbnails themselves are async on a QThread when file-backed (thumbnail_coordinator.py:167-169 moveToThread, get_pixmap at 67-69) but fall back to a GUI-thread one-page-per-turn live renderer (_render_live_page:201-225). utils/render_limits.py:5-18 caps a pixmap at 40 MP (safe_render_scale) but has no vector-complexity-aware throttle. No progressive/tile rendering exists. The MIC-VB-HVAC fixture is present (test_files/MIC-VB-HVAC-DWG-0001...pdf); git history shows only refactors touching it and generic perf (60c36fc async thumbnails, ecaf3d8 startup+large-PDF docs) - no fixture-specific vector optimization.
items.approach: Offload the primary page get_pixmap to a QThread+Signals worker (mirror thumbnail_coordinator's _ThumbnailWorker) so an AutoCAD page rasterizes off the GUI thread; keep the immediate low-res (scale<=0.5) placeholder path synchronous for instant feedback, then swap in the high-res result via signal. Optionally add tile/band rendering (render clipped rects of the page) and a complexity-aware scale cap layered onto safe_render_scale. Invalidate stale renders with the existing _render_gen_by_session tokens.
items.risk: High - page rendering is the central view path; threading interacts with the render cache, device-pixel-ratio keying (_render_device_pixel_ratio:653), color-profile invalidation and generation tokens; a bug shows stale/blank pages. Mitigate by reusing the proven thumbnail worker + gen-token cancellation and adding regression tests around _render_page_into_scene.
items.reuse: controller/thumbnail_coordinator.py worker (QThread, cancel via isInterruptionRequested); utils/render_limits.safe_render_scale; _render_cache + _render_cache_key; existing low/high two-tier quality machinery.

items.evidence: The 'touched precedence' model IS implemented and documented (docs/README.zh-TW.md:34). print_dialog.py tracks _touched_hardware_fields (74,355-358) and _resolve_hardware_values (469-495) only adds a hardware field to override_fields when the user changed it (paper_size/orientation always override when != 'auto'; duplex/color_mode only when touched). qt_bridge._apply_printer_options (159-175) applies duplex/color ONLY if in options.override_fields, while _set_page_layout (135-152) always applies paper/orientation. override_fields survives the helper-subprocess JSON boundary (helper_protocol.py:30 to_json_dict sorts it, :43 from_json_dict restores the set). The DEVMODE job-scoped set->print->restore path (win_driver._print_with_scoped_devmode:485-576) only runs when the user clicked Properties (print_dialog._open_printer_properties_dialog:370-405 captures _pending_devmode_buffer). Printing runs in a subprocess (print_coordinator._start_print_submission:260-303 + PrintSubprocessRunner). test_scripts/test_win_print_fixes.py covers DEVMODE job-scope/restore, mixed-media split, DPI cap, and findings #1-#11 - but has NO test reproducing 'fresh-boot, dialog options ignored, driver prefs used'.
items.approach: Reproduce with PDF_EDITOR_DEBUG logging around _resolve_hardware_values (dump override_fields) and the subprocess QPrinter application in qt_bridge._apply_printer_options; verify (a) _touched_hardware_fields actually populates on first boot given the _load_printers()->_on_printer_changed() call at print_dialog.py:291 vs _wire_signals ordering, (b) _syncing_printer_preferences (74) is not left True (it has try/finally at 346-351/392-405), and (c) whether, for UNtouched fields, the fresh QPrinter in the subprocess simply inherits the driver DEVMODE default (which is the documented design, not a bug). Add a red-light test that selects duplex/color/paper in the dialog and asserts override_fields reaches _apply_printer_options on a cold process. If the real cause is that untouched fields inherit driver defaults, the fix is a product decision (make dialog-shown values authoritative) rather than a code defect.
items.risk: Medium - changing whether untouched fields inherit driver defaults could regress the intentional touched-precedence contract and its existing tests; any fix must keep test_win_print_fixes.py green and add a first-boot regression test.
items.reuse: test_scripts/test_win_print_fixes.py fixtures (_FakeDispatcher, PyFakeHandle); existing override_fields/_touched_hardware_fields machinery; helper_protocol round-trip already correct.

items.evidence: Centering already exists: layout.compute_target_draw_rect (src/printing/layout.py:104-135) returns x=(tw-draw_w)/2, y=(th-draw_h)/2 (centered draw rect), and qt_bridge._draw_page_image (:178-196) uses it. However the centering target is printer.pageRect(QPrinter.Unit.DevicePixel) (qt_bridge.py:184) - the PRINTABLE area inside the driver's hardware margins - not the physical paperRect. On printers with asymmetric hardware margins, centering within the printable area leaves asymmetric margins relative to the physical paper edges, which can exceed the 2mm asymmetry target. The QPainter origin is at the pageRect top-left, and draw_rect is offset by target_rect.x()/y() (:195) but that offset is the pageRect origin, so content is centered in the printable box, not on paper.
items.approach: Center against the physical sheet: in _draw_page_image compute target from printer.paperRect(QPrinter.Unit.DevicePixel), center the image in that, and translate the draw rect by the printable-area origin so device coordinates stay valid (draw_rect = paper-centered rect minus pageRect origin). Alternatively set full-bleed margins via printer.setPageMargins(0,...)/setFullPage so pageRect ~= paperRect before centering. Add a unit test asserting |leftMargin-rightMargin| and |topMargin-bottomMargin| <= ~5.7pt (2mm).
items.risk: Low-medium - some drivers cannot print into the physical hardware-margin band, so full-bleed content may clip; keep fit/scale math (compute_target_draw_rect) intact and only change the centering reference rectangle.
items.reuse: compute_target_draw_rect already does the centering math; qt_bridge._draw_page_image is the single call site; test_win_print_fixes.py page-layout test scaffolding.

items.evidence: The app sets a Windows AppUserModelID (main.py:39-51 SetCurrentProcessExplicitAppUserModelID = APP_USER_MODEL_ID 'CyberSaga.CyberSagaPDF', app_identity.py:36) and app.setWindowIcon(load_app_icon()) (main.py:74). load_app_icon loads view/resources/app_icon.ico (view/icons.py:22,102-114). File association is done by scripts/windows_file_association.ps1, whose DefaultIcon defaults to view\resources\app_icon.ico (ps1:53,372-386) and whose launcher is a COPY of pythonw.exe named cybersaga_pdf.exe (ps1:12-13,63). The requirement's icon %SystemRoot%\System32\pdf_editor_icon(reiya).ico (to_Update.md:5) is NOT referenced anywhere in code - the app ships its own app_icon.ico. No PyInstaller .spec exists in the repo (Glob found only .venv/.../deploy_lib/default.spec), so there is no packaged EXE with an embedded reiya icon resource.
items.approach: Three distinct icon surfaces need fixing: (1) Taskbar/Alt-Tab: setWindowIcon+AUMID already wires this - ensure app_icon.ico (or the reiya .ico) contains all required sizes/PNG-compressed 256px and is valid (test_app_icon.py only asserts non-null). (2) File Explorer EXE icon: copying pythonw.exe (ps1:63) does NOT change its embedded icon resource, so Explorer shows Python's icon - fix by shipping a real launcher whose icon resource is the reiya .ico (PyInstaller build with icon=..., or a resource-patched exe). (3) Explorer .pdf file icon: the ProgID/Application DefaultIcon (ps1:372-386) must point at the reiya .ico AND the app must become the default handler (UserChoice, which the script deliberately cannot seize). Also flush the Windows icon cache (ie4uinit/iconcache). Point both view/icons.APP_ICON_PATH and the ps1 -IconPath at the exact reiya .ico (or vendor a byte-identical copy).
items.risk: Low - changes are packaging/registry/asset, not app logic; main runtime regression risk is a malformed .ico making load_app_icon return null (guarded at icons.py:109-114).
items.reuse: utils/app_identity (AUMID/APP single source), view/icons.load_app_icon, scripts/windows_file_association.ps1 (-IconPath param, snapshot/rollback registration); note to_Update.md:38 Pillow>=12.2.0+numpy is required before the PyInstaller rebuild that would embed the exe icon.

items.evidence: utils/single_instance.py already forwards the double-clicked file paths from the new process to the running one over a per-user QLocalServer/QLocalSocket (try_become_server:163, send_to_running_instance:215, JSON {argv} at :243). The new instance forwards then exits (main.py:120-127). The running instance handles the message via main.py handle_forwarded_paths:106-116 -> PDFController.handle_forwarded_cli (pdf_controller.py:1002-1014), which calls view.showNormal()/raise_()/activateWindow() (1007-1010). GAP: there is NO Windows foreground-lock workaround anywhere - grep for AllowSetForegroundWindow/SetForegroundWindow/AttachThreadInput/ASFW/FLASHW returns zero matches. Windows blocks SetForegroundWindow (which raise_/activateWindow rely on) for a process that did not originate the recent input, so the running window typically just flashes in the taskbar instead of coming to the foreground.
items.approach: Before the new (foreground-eligible) process forwards, call ctypes.windll.user32.AllowSetForegroundWindow(ASFW_ANY=-1) so the running instance is granted foreground rights; do this in single_instance.send_to_running_instance (win32-guarded, mirroring the platform guard style in main._set_windows_app_user_model_id:39-51). In the running instance's raise path (handle_forwarded_cli:1007-1010) add a robust bring-to-front fallback (e.g. brief SetWindowPos TOPMOST toggle or minimize+showNormal) for when foreground is still denied. Keep it within the 3s budget (forwarding already uses 2000ms timeouts).
items.risk: Low - additive, win32-only via ctypes with try/except; no layer impact (single_instance is a utils leaf, view raise stays in the view).
items.reuse: main._set_windows_app_user_model_id ctypes/win32-guard pattern; existing forwarding + handle_forwarded_cli raise attempt; QTimer.singleShot(0) dispatch already in place.

items.evidence: No recent-files/MRU feature exists - Grep for recent/mru/最近/recent_files/addRecent finds only unrelated hits (edit_commands.py, docs). utils/preferences.py (UserPreferences, 115 lines) is the QSettings facade to extend: _make_default_store uses QSettings(_ORG,_APP) (36-39) and get_*/set_* pairs (68-115) plus a one-time _migrate_legacy_settings (42-56). Menu-host gap: the app has NO File menu on Windows - _build_macos_menu_bar (view/pdf_view.py:1360-1374) returns False off darwin; the primary UI is the ribbon toolbar's '開啟' action -> sig_open_pdf (pdf_view.py:266). All open entry points funnel through PDFController.open_pdf (pdf_controller.py:955): toolbar Open -> _queue_or_open_paths (pdf_view.py:650-658) -> sig_open_pdf; drag-drop dropEvent (pdf_view.py:702-705) -> _queue_or_open_paths; single-instance forward -> handle_forwarded_cli (1002-1012); CLI arg (main.py:131-135). The forwarded-argv filter already requires an existing .pdf (single_instance.py:105-120); print temp dirs use tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='pdf_editor_print_') (print_coordinator.py:266).
items.approach: Add UserPreferences.get_recent_files()/add_recent_file(path) backed by QSettings (store a list, most-recent-first, dedup by canonical path via model._canonicalize_path, cap ~10, exclude paths under tempfile.gettempdir() and the pdf_editor_print_ spool prefix). Record once in PDFController.open_pdf after a successful model.open_pdf (pdf_controller.py:973) so every entry point is covered. Surface an MRU section: since there is no Windows menu bar, attach a QMenu to the toolbar '開啟' button (or add a small recents list), rebuilt when the list changes; on build, validate existence and drop/gray missing files (handle missing without crashing at open time via the existing FileNotFoundError path in model.open_pdf:698-700). Keep prefs writes in the controller (View emits sig_open_pdf; controller owns preferences), respecting the layer rule.
items.risk: Low - additive and read-mostly; main risks are recording spool/temp paths (excluded explicitly) and crashing on a deleted recent file (guarded by validating on menu build and the existing open-error path). Keep View->Controller via signals, not direct model access.
items.reuse: utils/preferences.py QSettings facade + _migrate pattern; app_identity ORG/APP; model._canonicalize_path (dedup); the single open_pdf funnel; single_instance's existing existing-.pdf filter.

platform-print-perf.notes: Requirements source is docs/to_Update.md (the live stale-able doc): item lines map as AC-FIX-01=>L2, AC-FIX-02=>L3, AC-FIX-03=>L4, AC-FIX-04=>L5, AC-NEW-02=>L23, AC-NEW-09=>L31; AC-MOD-04 corresponds to L12 (列印時要預設文件在紙張置中). .venv Pillow/numpy note (to_Update.md:38, 'Pillow 12.1.1, numpy missing') is relevant ONLY to AC-FIX-04 and any other packaging work: it gates the next PyInstaller rebuild, which is what would embed the reiya icon into the app EXE resource and match the secured requirements.txt; it has NO effect on run-from-source behavior of the other six items. No project PyInstaller .spec exists in the repo (only .venv/PySide6 deploy default.spec), which is itself a blocker for a proper Explorer EXE icon. Several to_Update.md bullets are OUT of the seven assigned items but share code paths: the thumbnail-not-showing bug (L6) and thumbnail centering (L10) live in thumbnail_coordinator/pdf_view; document centering (L11) is the view analogue of the print-centering fix (AC-MOD-04). AC-FIX-03 could not be root-caused by static reading alone - the touched-precedence machinery is implemented and unit-tested, so the fresh-boot symptom needs live reproduction; treat its status as partial-with-open-diagnosis rather than a located defect. AC-MOD-04 and centering are the closest to 'already largely implemented' (compute_target_draw_rect centers today; the only gap is pageRect-vs-paperRect reference)."

items.evidence: Corner-resize infra exists and already applies to textboxes with constant font size + reflow. Handles: view/object_selection.py:280-306 draws exactly 4 CORNER handles (TL/TR/BL/BR); view/pdf_view.py:123 compute_object_resize_rect only implements corner anchors 0-3 (no edge/midpoint anchors). Textbox eligibility: model/pdf_object_ops.py:630 supports_move=True; resize gated on supports_move at view/pdf_view.py:2862-2877; text_edit mode selects kind 'textbox' at view/pdf_view.py:2910. Constant-font reflow: resize_object (model/pdf_object_ops.py:967-977) delegates to move_object, which for textbox re-inserts _insert_textbox_visual_content at the SAME size=payload['size'] into the new rect (model/pdf_object_ops.py:724-747) => text reflows, font unchanged. Docs: FEATURES.md:36 documents corner-only resize; TODOS.md:139 lists resize as supported for textboxes in text-edit mode.
items.approach: Add 4 edge-midpoint handles in ObjectSelectionManager overlay (view/object_selection.py ~290-306) and extend view/pdf_view.py:123 compute_object_resize_rect with edge anchors that move only one axis (top/bottom => dy only, left/right => dx only), plus extend _hit_object_resize_handle_index (view/object_selection.py:311) to index them. No model change needed: move_object already reflows textbox text at constant font size into the destination rect. Wire edge-anchor indices through the existing _object_resize_handle_anchor path (view/pdf_view.py:2876, 3091).
items.risk: Edge-anchor geometry must interoperate with the existing aspect-lock branch (compute_object_resize_rect lines 156-180) and not regress corner resize; min-size clamping must apply per-axis. Covered by existing test_object_manipulation_gui.py / test_object_manipulation_model.py resize tests.
items.reuse: compute_object_resize_rect (view/pdf_view.py:123), ObjectSelectionManager handle draw/hit-test (view/object_selection.py:280-320), ResizeObjectRequest->move_object reflow path (model/pdf_object_ops.py:967, 724).

items.evidence: Float plumbing is done but manual-input + validation is largely absent. Control is a NON-editable QComboBox: view/pdf_view.py:1658-1660 (self.text_size = QComboBox(); addItems 8..28 step 2; no setEditable — only zoom_combo.setEditable(True) at view/pdf_view.py:1234). Parse/format helpers exist: _parse_font_size_str uses float() (view/text_editing.py:31), _format_font_size renders '9'/'9.5' with no trailing .0 (view/text_editing.py:39). Change handler on_edit_font_size_changed rejects None/<=0 by early return but does NOT restore last-valid text (view/text_editing.py:1528-1534). Sizes are float end-to-end into EditTextRequest.size (FEATURES.md:152 'Float font sizes throughout ... 9.5pt survive without truncation'); _sync_font_combo_state inserts arbitrary sizes (view/text_editing.py:1312-1323). float() currently accepts multi-decimal '9.55' with no one-decimal enforcement.
items.approach: Call self.text_size.setEditable(True) and attach a validator on its lineEdit (QDoubleValidator with 1 decimal, or a custom validator) enforcing exactly one decimal; validate on editingFinished/commit (not per-keystroke currentTextChanged). On blank/negative/multi-decimal, restore view._editing_current_pdf_size via setCurrentText(_format_font_size(...)). No model change: the float already reaches EditTextRequest.size at 0.1pt precision.
items.risk: Once editable, currentTextChanged (wired at view/text_editing.py:1332) fires on every keystroke — must gate validation/restore on commit to avoid mid-typing resets; low blast radius.
items.reuse: _parse_font_size_str/_format_font_size (view/text_editing.py:31,39), _editing_current_pdf_size, on_edit_font_size_changed (view/text_editing.py:1528), zoom_combo editable+validator pattern (view/pdf_view.py:1234).

items.evidence: Color-picker infra and undo/redo already exist; separate stroke/fill and border-width input are missing. Rect card UI: view/pdf_view.py:1609-1628 exposes a SINGLE rect_color (default #0078D4), rect_color_btn->_choose_rect_color via QColorDialog.getColor (view/pdf_view.py:1687-1693), rect_opacity QSlider, apply/cancel. Fill is chosen by a crude QMessageBox.question('是否填滿?') at view/pdf_view.py:4222, then sig_add_rect(page,rect,color,fill) emitted (:4223). Model add_rect uses ONE color for stroke+fill and HARDCODES border width: annotation_tool.py:58-77 (set_colors(stroke=color, fill=color if fill else None); set_border(width=5 if not fill else 0)). Undo/redo present: controller.add_rect wraps command_type='add_rect' (controller/pdf_controller.py:1586-1593). QColorDialog also used for highlight (_choose_highlight_color view/pdf_view.py:1698).
items.approach: Extend rect_card with a second QColorDialog button (stroke vs fill), a fill toggle QCheckBox (replace the QMessageBox modal at view/pdf_view.py:4222), and a QDoubleSpinBox border-width input (documented min/max/step) shown for no-fill rects. Widen sig_add_rect + controller.add_rect + annotation_tool.add_rect signatures to carry stroke_color, fill_color, fill, border_width; call annot.set_colors(stroke, fill) and annot.set_border(width=border_width). Preview parity via existing rect preview draw (_clear_rect_preview at view/pdf_view.py:4226).
items.risk: Signal-signature change ripples view->controller->model and touches add_rect tests (test_object_manipulation_model.py:70). Must ensure on-screen preview stroke width == saved set_border width (preview==output). Persisted rect payload (annotation_tool.py:64-71) should record border_width for round-trip/edit.
items.reuse: QColorDialog picker pattern (_choose_rect_color view/pdf_view.py:1687), rect_card widgets, AddRect undo command (controller/pdf_controller.py:1593), rect object payload marker (annotation_tool.py:64).

items.evidence: Text annotations today are INLINE FreeText boxes on the page, not Acrobat-style icon+popup. annotation_tool.add_annotation creates a 200x50 yellow FreeText annot at the click point (model/tools/annotation_tool.py:79-96). Flow: 'add_annotation' mode -> sig_add_annotation(page,doc_point,text) at view/pdf_view.py:2856 -> controller.add_annotation (controller/pdf_controller.py:2560-2574, undo command_type='add_annotation') -> annotation_tool.add_annotation. Listing via get_all_annotations scans FreeText (annotation_tool.py:98-115). No small label/icon rendering, no floating note window. Popup/proxy prior art: view.text_editor is a QGraphicsProxyWidget in-scene editor (view/text_editing.py:1049-1051, 1649-1652); modal QDialogs exist in view/dialogs/ (watermark.py, password.py, optimize.py); no frameless child note window exists.
items.approach: Render a small (<=32x32) marker item on the page (mirror the object-marker pattern _create_textbox_object_marker in model/pdf_object_ops.py, or a scene icon QGraphicsItem) instead of an inline text box. On marker click, open a frameless draggable QWidget/QDialog child of the main window (Qt.Tool/Qt.FramelessWindowHint, parented so it is not a taskbar window) hosting an editable QTextEdit; persist content to a PDF Text/FreeText+Popup annot. Make label and popup independently draggable (label drag => move marker rect via a move request; popup drag => window move). Route marker hit-testing so it works without colliding with browse/objects/text_edit selection.
items.risk: Requires an annotation identity/persistence model change (icon marker + popup content) and undo/redo integration; interaction-mode routing must decide which mode owns marker clicks. Encryption/save round-trip must stay on the controller chokepoint.
items.reuse: Object-marker creation pattern (model/pdf_object_ops.py _create_textbox_object_marker), QGraphicsProxyWidget infra (view/text_editing.py), add_annotation command/undo (controller/pdf_controller.py:2560), view/dialogs/ QDialog scaffolding.

items.evidence: No double-click handler and no word/token boundary function exist anywhere. Grep for mouseDoubleClickEvent/DoubleClick across the repo finds none in view/; grep get_words/word-boundary finds none. Text selection is drag-only: view/text_selection.py _start_text_selection (:69), _update_text_selection (:105), _finalize_text_selection (:160), all routed through controller.get_text_selection_lines using a start span_id + doc points (view/text_selection.py:129-135). Character-level data is available via model.get_chars_in_run (model/pdf_model.py:1917).
items.approach: Add PDFView.mouseDoubleClickEvent (browse mode only) that maps the scene pos to a page+doc point, resolves the char under the cursor via a new model helper built on get_chars_in_run / the block index, then expands left/right while chars stay in the numeric-token class (digits, single '.', ',', '/', a leading '-', excluding letters — covers A-123.45 stopping at the letter, 2026/07/02, ABC123DEF). Reuse get_text_selection_lines to render/finalize the resulting selection rect. Boundary rule is a new pure helper (e.g. _numeric_token_bounds(chars, hit_index)) that can be unit-tested independently.
items.risk: Char-box hit precision and cross-run tokens (a number split across runs); must not disturb existing drag-based selection or fire outside browse mode. Needs a new model char-at-point extraction path.
items.reuse: model.get_chars_in_run (model/pdf_model.py:1917), get_text_selection_lines + line-rect rendering (view/text_selection.py:129, _render_text_selection_line_rects:216).

items.evidence: Backend replay half-exists; no creation API and no UI mode. annotation_tool.py:23-24 defines _ANNOT_UNDERLINE=9/_ANNOT_STRIKEOUT=10; _restore_annots can replay them via page.add_underline_annot/add_strikeout_annot (annotation_tool.py:215-225). There is NO public add_underline/add_strikeout method (only add_highlight at annotation_tool.py:42). Highlight IS wired end-to-end: mode 'highlight' (controller _VALID_MODES controller/pdf_controller.py:197; view mode dispatch at view/pdf_view.py:4203-4211 emits sig_add_highlight) -> controller.add_highlight (controller/pdf_controller.py:1563) with undo command_type='add_highlight'. No underline/strikeout mode, signal, controller handler, or command. git log --grep=underline is empty (never landed).
items.approach: Mirror the highlight path exactly: add add_underline/add_strikeout to AnnotationTool (page.add_underline_annot/add_strikeout_annot, snapping to text via existing get_text_bounds at annotation_tool.py:50); add controller handlers + undo commands; add sig_add_underline/sig_add_strikeout signals; add two toolbar mode actions and mouse-release wiring analogous to the 'highlight' branch at view/pdf_view.py:4203; add the modes to view/controller _VALID_MODES.
items.risk: Low — parallels highlight. Must handle multi-line quads (add_*_annot(quads=...) vs rect) as _restore_annots already does (annotation_tool.py:216-220).
items.reuse: add_highlight + get_text_bounds (annotation_tool.py:42,50), highlight mode wiring (view/pdf_view.py:4203; controller/pdf_controller.py:1563), _restore_annots underline/strikeout branch (annotation_tool.py:215).

items.evidence: No user-facing get/set metadata wrapper and no dialog. The only metadata code is _blank_metadata_dict (model/pdf_model.py:2478 -> pdf_optimizer.blank_metadata_dict at model/pdf_optimizer.py:419), used internally by the optimizer which calls working_doc.set_metadata (model/pdf_optimizer.py:552). No model.get_metadata/set_metadata for user editing, no MetadataDialog (grep MetadataDialog -> none). PyMuPDF exposes doc.metadata (read) and doc.set_metadata (write).
items.approach: Add thin model.get_metadata()/set_metadata(dict) wrappers over doc.metadata/doc.set_metadata; build a small QDialog in view/dialogs/ (title/author/subject/keywords) following watermark.py; emit a signal -> controller command that calls set_metadata and records an undo entry (SnapshotCommand). Prefill from get_metadata.
items.risk: Undo must use a document snapshot; must route through the controller and preserve encryption via the established save chokepoint (recent security commits cbe6b9c/9f97aff). Not a page-content edit, so no text-index rebuild needed.
items.reuse: view/dialogs/ QDialog pattern (view/dialogs/watermark.py), controller command + SnapshotCommand undo infra, pdf_optimizer.blank_metadata_dict for field-name reference (model/pdf_optimizer.py:419).

items.evidence: Model does NOT wrap get_toc/set_toc (grep get_toc/set_toc across repo -> no model matches). A sidebar pattern to follow DOES exist: left sidebar is a QTabWidget built in _setup_left_sidebar (view/pdf_view.py:1526-1586) with tabs added via self.left_sidebar.addTab(widget, '縮圖'|'搜尋'|'註解列表'|'浮水印列表'); the thumbnail_list tab (view/pdf_view.py:1539) is the closest prior art for a click-to-navigate tree. PyMuPDF exposes doc.get_toc()/doc.set_toc().
items.approach: Add model.get_toc()/set_toc() wrappers; add a new QTreeWidget tab to left_sidebar via the existing addTab pattern (view/pdf_view.py:1526); on item click, jump to the target page reusing the thumbnail/search navigation path; add/rename/delete/reorder emit signals -> controller set_toc command with undo. PITFALLS stale-index note: TOC entries reference page numbers, so after page insert/delete the tree/toc page indices go stale and must be rebuilt/remapped (same class of issue tracked for the text index).
items.risk: Undo of set_toc plus keeping the tree consistent after page insert/delete (stale page indices). Must stay View->signal->Controller->Model; no direct model calls from the tree widget.
items.reuse: left_sidebar addTab + thumbnail_list navigation (view/pdf_view.py:1526-1586), search jump/navigation pattern (controller/search_coordinator.py), SnapshotCommand undo.

items.evidence: Interaction-mode split is established but has a view/controller mismatch a plan must respect. TODOS.md:131-141 'Notes on objects mode': browse=text selection; objects mode=rectangles + app images + native images; text-edit mode=textboxes (move/rotate/delete/resize/multi-select + word editing); identity layer shared. Enforced in code: view/pdf_view.py:2910 sets allowed_kinds=('rect','image','native_image') for objects vs ('textbox',) for text_edit. IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY: view _VALID_MODES includes 'objects' and 'text_edit' (view/pdf_view.py:243) but controller _VALID_MODES does NOT (controller/pdf_controller.py:197 = browse/edit_text/add_text/rect/highlight/add_annotation).
items.approach: Respect these routing constraints when planning items 1-4: (1) AC-MOD-06 textbox edge-resize lives in text_edit mode only (not objects); (2) AC-MOD-08 rect stroke/fill/border UI belongs to objects-mode selection + the 'rect' drawing mode; (3) AC-MOD-09 annotation markers are a separate identity from the object layer (rect/image/textbox) and their click hit-test must be routed so it does not steal browse text-selection or objects/text_edit selection; (4) AC-NEW-04 double-click numeric token must fire in browse mode only. Any new mode (e.g. underline/strikeout) must be added to BOTH view/pdf_view.py:243 and controller/pdf_controller.py:197.
items.risk: Mode-routing regressions if a new interaction (marker click, double-click) is not gated by current_mode; the view/controller _VALID_MODES divergence can silently reject a mode on one side. Covered by test_interaction_modes.py.
items.reuse: set_mode + current_mode dispatch (view/pdf_view.py:1859-1934), allowed_kinds gate (view/pdf_view.py:2910), test_interaction_modes.py mode-gating tests.

editing-tools.notes: Cross-cutting observations: (1) AC-MOD-06 is the closest to done of the 'missing' items — corner resize already reflows textbox text at constant font size (model move_object re-inserts at payload['size']); only EDGE (single-dimension midpoint) handles are absent, so this is a view-layer-only add. (2) AC-MOD-07's float pipeline is fully in place (FEATURES.md:152); the real gap is that the font-size QComboBox is not even editable and lacks a validator/restore-last-valid, so 'manual input' cannot happen today. (3) AC-MOD-08 already has QColorDialog + AddRect undo; the fill choice currently uses a crude QMessageBox.question modal (view/pdf_view.py:4222) and border width is hardcoded to 5/0 in annotation_tool.add_rect. (4) Two potential stale/gotcha items for a plan author: the view vs controller _VALID_MODES divergence (view has objects/text_edit, controller does not — controller/pdf_controller.py:197 vs view/pdf_view.py:243), and note there are BOTH 'edit_text' and 'text_edit' modes which are distinct — do not conflate them. (5) Underline/strikeout, metadata editor, and bookmarks/TOC are all genuinely un-started at the feature level (git history confirms: --grep=underline empty; no MetadataDialog; no model get_toc/set_toc), but each has a clean prior-art path to mirror (highlight wiring, view/dialogs QDialog, left_sidebar addTab + thumbnail navigation respectively). (6) Advisor tool was unavailable during this recon, so these findings were not externally cross-checked.
